objective: Add concise one-line docstrings to public test functions and fixtures in the core test modules flagged by the scanner, covering tests/test_agent_token_tracking.py, tests/test_registry.py, tests/test_pipeline_config.py, tests/test_workspace_typing.py, tests/test_smoke.py, tests/test_basic.py, tests/test_agent_result.py, tests/test_history.py, tests/test_cli_doctor.py, tests/test_module_docstrings.py, tests/test_identity.py, tests/test_cli_compare.py, tests/test_dependencies.py, tests/test_event_bus.py, and tests/test_surgical_mode_cli_and_controller.py.

plan_steps.description: Add concise one-line docstrings to every public pytest test, fixture, and any public test helper methods flagged in the first batch of core test modules, inserting the docstring immediately below each existing function signature without changing assertions, names, decorators, or test flow.
Add short descriptive docstrings to the public tests in the smoke/basic and result/history-oriented core test modules, preserving existing behavior and collection exactly.

Add one-line docstrings to public tests, fixtures, and any public setup helpers in the CLI and module-docstring-focused test modules; if a fixture helper exposes test-only methods like run() or model_dump(), document those stub methods in place without altering their return values or behavior.

Add concise docstrings to all scanner-flagged public tests, fixtures, and public helper methods in the dependency, event bus, and surgical-mode controller test modules, keeping imports, assertions, and monkeypatch setup unchanged.
